DataFrame conversion is now a chain on the returned list (`tdx.stock_history_eod(...).to_polars()`). Every list-returning endpoint wraps its result in a typed `<ReturnType>List` pyclass exposing `.to_polars()`, `.to_arrow()`, `.to_pandas()`, `.to_list()` and the list protocol, so there is exactly one surface for converting tick data into a DataFrame. The free-function and client-method `to_polars(ticks)` / `to_arrow(ticks)` / `to_pandas(ticks)` / `to_dataframe(ticks)` helpers are removed hard.

See the [v8.0.3 entry in the structured changelog](https://github.com/userFRM/ThetaDataDx/blob/v8.0.3/CHANGELOG.md#803---2026-04-22) for the full list.

## Changed

- **Chained DataFrame conversion on every list-returning endpoint.** Every endpoint now returns a typed `<ReturnType>List` pyclass (`EodTickList`, `TradeTickList`, `QuoteTickList`, …, plus `StringList`, `OptionContractList`, `CalendarDayList` for non-tick list returns). The wrapper exposes `.to_polars()`, `.to_arrow()`, `.to_pandas()`, `.to_list()` and the full list protocol. Usage is `tdx.stock_history_eod(...).to_polars()` — no intermediate variable, no free-function round-trip.
- **Builder terminals collapse to a single `.list()`.** Previously `.list()` / `.arrow()` / `.pandas()` / `.polars()` lived as four parallel terminals on each builder. The DataFrame terminals now live on the returned list wrapper, so builder usage is `tdx.stock_history_eod_builder(...).list().to_polars()`. One code path, one place to audit, no four-way drift.

## Removed (breaking)

- **`thetadatadx.to_polars(ticks)` / `to_arrow(ticks)` / `to_pandas(ticks)` / `to_dataframe(ticks)` removed.** Both the free-function entry points and the identically-named methods on the client handle are deleted. Migration: chain the terminal off the endpoint return value.
  ```python
  # Before (8.0.2)
  ticks = tdx.stock_history_eod(symbol, start, end)
  df = thetadatadx.to_polars(ticks)

  # After (8.0.3)
  df = tdx.stock_history_eod(symbol, start, end).to_polars()
  ```
  The same rewrite applies to `to_arrow` / `to_pandas` / `to_dataframe`. No compatibility shim ships — migration is mechanical and the diff is the minimum needed to move off the deleted surface.

## Internal

- **DRY async emit — generator-emitted `_async` methods delegate to a `spawn_awaitable` helper** mirroring the sync `run_blocking` pattern. One call per emit replaces the open-coded `pyo3_async_runtimes::tokio::future_into_py(...)` + `Python::attach` + `map_err(to_py_err)` scaffolding every `_async` method previously inlined. `sdks/python/src/historical_methods.rs` sheds ~599 lines of duplicated plumbing; the helper itself lives in `sdks/python/src/async_runtime.rs` and is covered by unit tests for the error-mapping + GIL-attach invariants.
- **Docs-site restructure.** Deleted the standalone benchmark page, the migration-from-thetadata guide, the five per-language `quickstart/*.md` files, and the separate async-python narrative. Replaced with a unified code-group quickstart exposing Rust / Python / TypeScript / Go / C++ via language tabs so a single page stays in sync across SDKs.

## Release notes

- Every Rust crate version bumps `8.0.2 → 8.0.3`: `thetadatadx`, `thetadatadx-ffi`, `thetadatadx-cli`, `thetadatadx-server`, `thetadatadx-mcp`, `thetadatadx-py`, `thetadatadx-napi`.
- `sdks/typescript/package.json` + every platform subpackage under `sdks/typescript/npm/` bumps to `8.0.3` so the npm dependency graph stays coherent.
- `tdbe` stays at `0.11.0` (no API change in this release — all customer-visible surface lives in `thetadatadx` and its downstream SDKs).
- Verification: `cargo fmt --all -- --check`, `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings`, `cargo test --workspace`, `bash crates/thetadatadx/tests/regen_byte_identical.sh`, `cargo run -p thetadatadx --features config-file --bin generate_sdk_surfaces --locked -- --check`, `python3 scripts/check_docs_consistency.py`, `cd docs-site && npm install && npm run build` all pass.
